text: A 6-day taper with daily training (high frequency) and 80% nonlinear progressive reduction in high-intensity interval training volume significantly improves 800m performance in middle-distance runners.
Resting every third day during a 6-day taper (moderate frequency) does not produce significant performance improvements, unlike training daily throughout the taper.
Maintaining training frequency during a taper is more important for performance gains than inserting rest days, even when training volume is reduced by 80%.
Total testosterone increases significantly during a 6-day taper, indicating a shift toward an anabolic hormonal environment.
Peak blood lactate capacity increases significantly after a 6-day taper and correlates with improved performance, suggesting enhanced anaerobic capacity.
Changes in peak blood lactate during taper correlate strongly with the testosterone:cortisol ratio, linking hormonal recovery to anaerobic performance capacity.
